msgs.text:
Turn 33 — H5(2)dies structurally; so do H6(2)and H7(2)I re-read only the files attached to this turn. The problem remains the over-G isomorphism question for the two quotients ΓS,ΓH defined from F=P⋊G and relators rS,rH, with the required condition πH∘Φ=πS.

The new point is:

The next requested A2 -secondary test H5(2)is automatically killed.

In fact, the same argument kills H6(2)and H7(2). So the next genuinely new A2=R/(4,t) secondary coefficient test is not d=5, but d=8.Recall the state after Turn 32. The first t=s−1 coefficient layer R/(2,t2) is contractible, while the remaining first coefficient issue is the A2=R/(4,t) secondary towerHd(2):=Ld[2]/imθd0.

Turn 30 computeddimHd(2)=1,3,7,12,21,43,81(d=4,…,10),and Turn 31 killed the first scalar H4(2). Turn 32 therefore suggested computing the actual defect in H5(2)and testing it moduloB5:kerD6,7,8,9→H5(2).But this is exactly where we should step back. H5(2)is only a quotient of L5[2]. The earlier residual sweep already proved a stronger statement at the corresponding square degree 10:ker∂8↠L10/im∂9≅L5[2].This was the decisive Turn 7 mechanism: degree 10 was not killed by testing ∂9 alone, but by the degree-8 kernel freedom mapping onto the entire six-dimensional square quotient L5[2].Now observe that these same degree-8 kernel directions are legitimate A2 -coefficient continuation directions for B5. They begin in degree 8, so they do not affect the lower root-degree 5 coefficient ghost. They also lie in ker∂8, so they create no residual degree-9 tail. Therefore they embed into the continuation kernel kerD6,7,8,9. Their coefficient-Bockstein shadow is the same square-moving map already used in the residual degree-10 calculation.Letπ5:L5[2]↠H5(2)be the quotient map. ThenimB5⊇π5 (im(ker∂8→L5[2]))=π5(L5[2])=H5(2).

HenceB5↠H5(2).

Since dimH5(2)=3, this says rankB5=3. The actual H5(2)-defect does not need to be computed: every possible class in H5(2)is already movable by the residual degree-8 kernel directions.The same argument also kills the next two secondary coefficient layers.For d=6, Turn 8 proved that the full affine kernel through degree 11 maps ontoL12/im∂11≅L6[2].Those corrections begin in degrees 8,9,10, preserve the lower residual layers through degree 11, and therefore sit inside the A2 -continuation kernel relevant to B6. Composing with L6[2]↠H6(2)givesB6↠H6(2).

So the 7-dimensional H6(2)obstruction space is also fully killed.For d=7, Turn 9 proved that the full affine kernel through degree 13 maps ontoL14/im∂13≅L7[2].

Again these directions are available inside the A2 -continuation kernel for B7, and composing with L7[2]↠H7(2)givesB7↠H7(2).

Thus the 12-dimensional H7(2)obstruction space is killed as well.

So the useful structural statement of this turn is:Bd↠Hd(2)for d=5,6,7.

This is stronger than checking the actual H5(2)-defect. It shows that the first three post-μ4A2 -secondary coefficient spaces are automatically harmless because the earlier residual square-surjectivity maps already dominate them.The next new bottleneck is d=8. Here the residual degree-16 story was exceptional: the high torsor alone had codimension one in L8[2], and the missing direction was supplied only after lower I9 -base retuning. Turn 14 showed this lower retuning kills the residual degree-16 obstruction, but the coefficient-level question is subtler because the A2 -secondary target is nowH8(2)=L8[2]/imθ80,dimH8(2)=21.

Thus the next test should not be a fixed H8 -tail. It should ask whether the combined map (degree 8,…,15 high kernel)+(zero-2-ghost lower I9 -base retunings)⟶H8(2)is onto. If it is onto, then the entire first A2 -secondary tower through d=8 is cleared. If it has a codimension-one image, then the actual H8(2)-defect must be paired with the remaining functional.

ESTABLISHED: The A2=R/(4,t) secondary maps satisfyB5↠H5(2),B6↠H6(2),B7↠H7(2).The proof is structural: the residual square-surjectivity maps at degrees 10,12,14 already hit all of L5[2],L6[2],L7[2], and the A2 -secondary spaces are quotients of these. Therefore the actual H5(2),H6(2),H7(2)coefficient defects cannot survive.

GAP: This still does not prove finite coefficient nonemptiness over R≃Z2 [[t]]. The next genuinely new A2 -secondary bottleneck is H8(2), because residual degree 16 required lower-base retuning rather than high-kernel surjectivity alone. Higher m=(2,t)-adic layers such as 4,2t,t2 also remain outside the first-layer check.

NEXT: Test the combined d=8 coefficient map toH8(2)=L8[2]/imθ80 including both the residual high kernel from degrees 8,…,15 and the zero-2-ghost lower I9 -base retunings, especially the section-cocycle q-directions that previously supplied missing square shadows.[TURN33-H5-H7-A2-SECONDARY-CLEARED]
